title: Global Criminal Justice Act
plain_summary: This bill creates a new office in the US State Department to fight serious crimes around the world. The office will focus on terrible crimes like war crimes, genocide, and crimes against humanity. It will help make policies, work with international courts, and coordinate efforts to stop these crimes from happening. The office will also work to make sure people who commit these crimes get punished.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Global Criminal Justice Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Office of Global Criminal Justice
sections.text: The Department of State must create a new office called the Office of Global Criminal Justice. The Secretary of State can decide where to put this office within the department. This office has several important jobs. First, it must give advice to the Secretary of State and other top government officials about terrible crimes like war crimes, crimes against humanity, and genocide (the killing of large groups of people because of their race, religion, or nationality). Second, it must help create policies for preventing these terrible crimes, responding when they happen, and making sure the people who commit them are punished. Third, it must work with other government departments to coordinate what the United States says about international courts that are putting people on trial for these crimes around the world. Fourth, it must work with other countries, international organizations, and private groups to set up committees and courts that investigate and prosecute these terrible crimes worldwide. Fifth, it must coordinate with other government departments to use diplomatic, legal, economic, military, and other tools to gather evidence of these crimes, judge those responsible, protect and help victims, help communities heal, prevent future crimes, and promote fair laws. Sixth, it must provide advice and knowledge about justice systems to United States workers in areas where there is conflict or where conflict recently ended. Seventh, it must serve as the main contact point for courts around the world that handle cases about these terrible crimes. Eighth, it must represent the Department of State in any government-wide groups that work on genocide and other terrible crimes. Ninth, it must do any other jobs that the Secretary of State assigns. This office will be run by an Ambassador-at-Large for Global Criminal Justice. The President must nominate this person, and the Senate must approve the appointment.
